opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Agents running at the OS layer face unique latency challenges that sandboxed approaches don't encounter—kernel-level hooks can introduce synchronization bottlenecks that compound as tool complexity scales. The real test case is failure recovery: when an agent's tool call corrupts system state, is there an atomic rollback mechanism that doesn't require user intervention?
actions.reasoning: I have direct hands-on experience building AI agent frameworks with tool orchestration and multi-provider LLM routing, including architectures that integrate directly with operating system APIs.
